summary: A short, cinematic trailer-style hype edit promoting a UFC 'FIGHT NIGHT' on the White House South Lawn for America's 250th anniversary. It intercuts dramatic black title cards in thin serif type ('PRESIDENT DONALD T...', 'THE WHITE HOUSE') with two muscular UFC fighters in tailored suits squaring off on the South Lawn in front of the White House, plus glossy hero shots of a UFC 'FREEDOM 250' championship belt and program emblazoned with stars and stripes. Music-driven with no dialogue, it borrows fight-promo and movie-trailer aesthetics to make a presidential event feel like a blockbuster sporting spectacle aimed squarely at a young, hype-driven audience.

script.scene: Black screen with faint thin-serif title text resolving (reads like 'President Donald T...'), trailer-style cold open over music.
Two muscular UFC fighters in fitted suits (one navy, one tan) stand side by side on the South Lawn, the White House behind them, gazing off heroically.
The two fighters turn to face each other in a tense face-off stance on the lawn, White House and a red-brick building in the background.

Glossy close-up of a UFC 'FREEDOM 250' program/booklet with red-white-blue stars logo on a reflective wood table; quick cut to a gold UFC championship belt with a stars-and-stripes faceplate, overlaid with faint trailer text.
Black title card with the line-drawn Capitol/White House emblem and bold text 'THE WHITE HOUSE' to close the promo.

notable_text: Tweet: 'America's epic 250 summer loading... @UFC FIGHT NIGHT ON THE SOUTH LAWN'
